issue_title: [Feature] Redirect video stream to new path
issue_body:
Some of us run Playwright as test cases, e.g. via Jest using `it` statements and test files (suites). When using [the awesome videos functionality](https://playwright.dev/docs/videos) it saves one huge video in case the browser context never closes between tests.

It would be cool to have hooks to `page.video()` e.g. `page.video().path('some-path')` to redirect the buffer to a new file. It would also be cool if I can chose the file name of the video in those scenarios, as I might want to name them as the test case. (I can add a UUID or similar myself if wanted).
pr_body:
These methods are safe to call while the page is still open, or when it is
already closed. Works in remotely connected browser as well.

Also makes `video.path()` to throw for remotely connected browser.

Under the hood migrated Download and Video to use the common Artifact object.

Fixes #5853.
